identities.description:
Identities holds the list of patches to apply

required

config.description: Create Identity and Import Lookup Secret Credentials Configuration

codes.description: Codes is a list of "lookup secret" codes configured for the user.

items.description: Recovery codes can be used once and are invalidated after use.
items.title: RecoveryCode represents one recovery code associated with a lookup_secret credential.

config.title: AdminIdentityImportCredentialsLookupSecretConfig

oidc.description: Create Identity and Import Social Sign In Credentials

providers.description: A list of OpenID Connect Providers

items.description: Create Identity and Import Social Sign In Credentials Configuration

provider.description: The OpenID Connect provider to link the subject to. Usually something like `google` or `github`.

subject.description: The subject (`sub`) of the OpenID Connect connection. Usually the `sub` field of the ID Token.

use_auto_link.description: If set, this credential allows the user to sign in using the OpenID Connect provider without setting the subject first.

config.description: Create Identity and Import Passkey Credentials Configuration

attestation.title: CredentialWebAuthnAttestation contains information about attestation of a webauthn credential.

authenticator.title: AuthenticatorWebAuthn contains information about the authenticator of a webauthn credential.

flags.title: CredentialWebAuthnFlags contains information about the flags of a webauthn credential.

items.description:
Authenticators may implement various transports for communicating with clients. This enumeration defines hints as to
how clients might communicate with a particular authenticator in order to obtain an assertion for a specific
credential. Note that these hints represent the WebAuthn Relying Party's best belief as to how an authenticator may
be reached. A Relying Party will typically learn of the supported transports for a public key credential via
getTransports().

Specification: §5.8.4. Authenticator Transport Enumeration (https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#enumdef-authenticatortransport)
items.title: AuthenticatorTransport represents the IDL enum with the same name.
CredentialWebAuthn contains information about a webauthn credential.

user_handle.description: UserHandle is the user handle of the webauthn credential.

config.title: identityWithCredentialsPasskeyConfig

password.description: The password in plain text if no hash is available.

use_password_migration_hook.description: If set to true, the password will be migrated using the password migration hook.

password.title: identityWithCredentialsPassword

provider.description: The SAML provider to link the subject to.

subject.description: The unique subject of the SAML connection. This value must be immutable at the source.

items.description:
Authenticators may implement various transports for communicating with clients. This enumeration defines hints as to
how clients might communicate with a particular authenticator in order to obtain an assertion for a specific
credential. Note that these hints represent the WebAuthn Relying Party's best belief as to how an authenticator may
be reached. A Relying Party will typically learn of the supported transports for a public key credential via

Specification: §5.8.4. Authenticator Transport Enumeration (https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#enumdef-authenticatortransport)

webauthn.title: identityWithCredentialsWebAuthn

external_id.description:
ExternalID is an optional external ID of the identity. This is used to link
the identity to an external system. If set, the external ID must be unique
across all identities.

metadata_admin.description: Store metadata about the user which is only accessible through admin APIs such as `GET /admin/identities/<id>`.

metadata_public.description:
Store metadata about the identity which the identity itself can see when calling for example the
session endpoint. Do not store sensitive information (e.g. credit score) about the identity in this field.

recovery_addresses.description:
RecoveryAddresses contains all the addresses that can be used to recover an identity.

Use this structure to import recovery addresses for an identity. Please keep in mind
that the address needs to be represented in the Identity Schema or this field will be overwritten
on the next identity update.

created_at.description: CreatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

region.description:
Region is the Ory Network region this identity will be created in.
Optional; defaults to the project home region if omitted. Only effective
on the Ory Network.
eu-central EUCentral
asia-northeast AsiaNorthEast
us-east USEast
us-west USWest
eu EU
asia Asia
us US
global Global
region.x-go-enum-desc:
eu-central EUCentral
asia-northeast AsiaNorthEast
us-east USEast
us-west USWest
eu EU
asia Asia
us US
global Global

schema_id.description: SchemaID is the ID of the JSON Schema to be used for validating the identity's traits.

state.description:
State is the identity's state.
active StateActive
inactive StateInactive

traits.description:
Traits represent an identity's traits. The identity is able to create, modify, and delete traits
in a self-service manner. The input will always be validated against the JSON Schema defined
in `schema_url`.

verifiable_addresses.description:
VerifiableAddresses contains all the addresses that can be verified by the user.

Use this structure to import verified addresses for an identity. Please keep in mind
that the address needs to be represented in the Identity Schema or this field will be overwritten
on the next identity update.

items.description: VerifiableAddress is an identity's verifiable address

status.description: VerifiableAddressStatus must not exceed 16 characters as that is the limitation in the SQL Schema
status.title: identityVerifiableAddressStatus

updated_at.description: When this entry was last updated

verified.description: Indicates if the address has already been verified

items.title: verifiableIdentityAddress

patch_id.description:
The ID of this patch.

The patch ID is optional. If specified, the ID will be returned in the
response, so consumers of this API can correlate the response with the
patch.
